title: Quality & Hse Operational Engineer
description:
**Job Title: Quality \& Hse Operational Engineer**

**Location: Ronchi dei Legionari \- Italy**

**Travel: Yes**

**Company: Munters Corporation – DCT**

**About Us:**


Munters is a global leader in climate solutions for mission\-critical processes. We offer innovative, efficient and sustainable solutions for customers in industries where controlling indoor humidity, temperature and energy efficiency is mission critical. Climate control systems often account for a large percentage of energy consumption in many of our customers’ operations. With an optimal climate system, we can help them to more efficiently use energy or water resources and thereby reduce their climate and environmental impact. Sustainability is an important part of Munters’ business strategy and value creation. We pride ourselves on fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment where interns are valued members of the team and are given meaningful projects to work on

**Primary Job Summary:**


The Quality \& Hse Operational Engineer role is responsible for supporting the QHSE Manager in the daily management of quality, safety, and operational processes, ensuring the correct implementation of company procedures and the Quality \& HSE system. The position provides hands\-on support across production and quality activities, including non\-conformance management, document control, and process monitoring. The role collaborates closely with production, suppliers, and internal stakeholders to resolve issues, ensure compliance with applicable standards, and maintain operational effectiveness. The position also contributes to KPI tracking, reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives, while promoting a strong quality and safety culture across the organization.

**Duties and responsibilities:**


This role includes

**Quality \& Production Support**


This role includes supporting the QHSE Manager in the daily execution of quality and HSE activities, ensuring the correct application of procedures, work instructions, and control plans across production.

**Non\-Conformance Management**


The position is responsible for managing and monitoring internal and external non\-conformances, performing preliminary root cause analysis, and coordinating corrective actions with production and suppliers.

**Quality System \& Documentation**


The role supports the maintenance and update of quality system documentation in line with applicable standards (e.g., ISO 9001, PED, ASME), contributes to internal and external audits, and ensures proper record\-keeping and data collection for compliance purposes. It also involves monitoring technical deadlines, equipment, PPE, and system certifications.

**Health, Safety \& Environment (HSE)**


In terms of safety, the role assists in ensuring compliance with HSE procedures on the shop floor, supports the collection and analysis of near\-miss and incident data, and coordinates with external providers for medical surveillance and mandatory training.

**Data Analysis \& Continuous Improvement**


Additionally, the position contributes to data collection and analysis of quality and safety KPIs, monitors scrap, rework, and process deviations, and supports the implementation of corrective actions and continuous improvement initiatives, fostering a strong quality and safety culture throughout the organization.

**Skills and qualifications:**

* Experience supporting operational quality and HSE activities in a production or industrial environment.
* Knowledge of quality standards (ISO 9001, PED, ASME), HSE regulations, and production processes.
* Capable of managing multiple priorities while addressing operational matters efficiently and remaining calm under pressure.
* Strong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills, with the ability to work independently as well as collaboratively in a team environment.
* Proficient in Microsoft Office and adaptable to learning new software and reporting tools as needed.

**Education Requirements:**

* High school diploma (Technical Institute or Scientific) or a university degree; additional specialization, professional course, or a master’s degree
* Relevant experience in operational quality and HSE support, preferably in a production or industrial environment.
* Proven ability to read and interpret technical documentation, procedures, and regulatory standards.
* Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work autonomously and in a team.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office; familiarity with industry\-specific software is a plus.

**Benefits:**

* Competitive Salary
* Professional development opportunities
* State\-of\-the\-art equipment and tools to support your work

**Equal Opportunity Employer:**

Munters are equal opportunity employers and are committed to diversity in the workplace. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or veteran status.

**Interested to join?**


If you are excited about this opportunity, please submit your application as soon as possible


To help us place the right people in the right role, we believe in we follow a competence\-based, structured interview process where your skills, knowledge and attributes relevant to Munters will be assessed.


At Munters, you get the chance to influence and solve meaningful problems for society . We lead with an obsession for climate, with people in the core and sustainability as part of the DNA.
company_description: Munters is a global leader in climate control solutions for mission-critical processes.

title: Sales & Clinical Specialist - Emilia-Romagna
description:
In HARTMANN, siamo tutti impegnati ad **aiutare, prenderci cura, proteggere** e **crescere**. Supportiamo gli operatori sanitari affinché possano concentrarsi su ciò che conta davvero: avere un impatto positivo sulla vita delle persone. Realizziamo soluzioni che fanno la differenza. E con il tuo impegno potrai crescere ogni giorno nel tuo lavoro. Noi di HARTMANN crediamo nella differenza che puoi fare.

  

Unisciti al nostro team come**Sales \& Clinical Specialist \- Emilia\-Romagna**
ITA\-Verona  

HARTMANN Italia, la filiale italiana del Gruppo HARTMANN (azienda tedesca del settore medicale, quotata in borsa), per la BU Incontinence, è alla ricerca di un **Sales \& Clinical Specialist**.  

**Responsabilità:**

  

* Gestisce in prima persona un portafoglio clienti con i quali negozia personalmente
* Implementa sulla propria area di vendita le strategie definite dalla Direzione
* Concorda i progetti nelle strutture, nonché la partecipazione alle gare, con il proprio superiore diretto
* Informa preventivamente l’azienda sui piani di lavoro settimanali
* Riceve ed eroga competenza medica sui prodotti di propria competenza
* Tiene seminari ai clienti
* Tiene corsi di aggiornamento alla rete vendita
* Realizza test clinici sui prodotti
* Indaga e documenta le evidenze cliniche dei prodotti medicali
* Affianca i funzionari di vendita per argomentare in chiave tecnico\-scientifica le funzioni di un prodotto
* Esegue sperimentazioni finalizzate alle gare
* Riporta al sales manager l’esito dei progetti affidati
* Organizza simposi su temi concordati con l’azienda

 **Qualificazioni:**

  

* Laurea in Scienze Infermieristiche o materie scientifiche;
* Pregressa esperienza nella vendita di medical devices nel Canale Ospedagliero o RSA
* Conoscenza nel mercato in cui si opera e delle relative dinamiche commerciali
* Doti negoziali, relazionali e di mediazione
* Forte orientamento al risultato e al raggiungimento dei target di vendita e marginalità assegnati
* Capacità di combinare l’argomentazione tecnica con quella commerciale
* Capacità di presentare un’offerta
* Doti organizzative
* Disponibilità alla mobilità territoriale nell’area assegnata
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Office

 **Cosa offriamo:**  

* Azienda internazionale stabile e in continua crescita con oltre 200 anni di storia
* Un ambiente di lavoro innovativo e dinamico
* Formazione continua per supportarti nello sviluppo professionale
* Assunzione diretta con l’azienda, CCNL Commercio
* Auto ad uso promiscuo, iPad, iPhone e pc aziendale
* Un pacchetto incentivante al raggiungimento di obiettivi
* Varie offerte per i dipendenti, ad es. Piattaforma di benefit aziendali e varie offerte di salute e fitness

 **Dipartimento:** Sales

**Inserimento:** immediato

**Area di lavoro:** Emilia\-Romagna

  

Dai forma a una carriera focalizzata sui clienti del settore sanitario, adatta al futuro e fondata su una cultura di fiducia e apertura. **Candidati ora** tramite la nostra piattaforma online.
company_description: To drive healthcare forward, we have a passion for what we do. We are proud of our achievements, but we won't rest. After all, there are always more lives that can be improved by our continued work.

title: Warehousing Coordinator
description:
**Who are we?**

At Vertiv, we’re on a mission to empower the people that will power the future. From a simple swipe to life\-changing medicines, from push notifications to generative AI. We design, manufacture, and service the products and solutions that keep the world connected. With $6\.9 billion in sales, a strong customer base and global reach spanning nearly 70 countries, we are uniquely positioned to deliver greater value to our customers and create new opportunities for our people.  



We are currently seeking a **Warehousing Coordinator** to join our dynamic team in **Piove di Sacco (PD), Italy!**

Manage office administrative and accounting activities in collaboration with the Warehouse / Shipping Manager, with the aim of achieving and maintaining efficiency and effectiveness targets, in compliance with safety and environmental standards. This includes ensuring inventory, accounting, and procedural accuracy within the required volumes, order fulfillment lead times, and quality standards.  



**What kind of work will you be doing?**

* Execute and monitor IT transactions on ERP and WMS (Warehouse Management System), managing any anomalies (e.g. inbound and outbound transactions, picking, delivery notes, customs documentation requests, transfers, inventories, locations, Non\-Conformities).
* Manage and maintain the archive of the above\-mentioned documents, ensuring it is up to date and properly organized.
* Handle, from an administrative and accounting perspective, non\-conformities, subcontracting accounts, consignment stock, and loan\-for\-use processes, in compliance with current regulations and internal procedures.
* Interface with internal departments such as Production, Purchasing, Engineering, Administration, Accounting, and Quality, as well as with suppliers, based on received information and defined procedures.
* Collaborate with the Manager in coordinating Warehouse and Shipping activities, entering the required information into logistics partners’ portals to execute shipments, and liaising with OPC and/or other departments to ensure the timely dispatch of documents and goods.
* Specifically, manage the CSW Service Spare Parts Warehouse, including spare parts shipments across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa for all Vertiv products, in cooperation with the Central Spares Logistics team in Bratislava, ensuring a high level of service to customers within agreed timelines.
* Support the monitoring and management of warehouse KPIs, including scrap value, inventory turnover, and inventory accuracy.
* Support safety and environmental activities, both in terms of documentation and supervision of compliance with departmental procedures.
Operate in accordance with ITC regulations, meeting role requirements related to Export Compliance, Import Compliance, EU Arrivals and Dispatching, and Supply Chain Security.  
* 

**What will make you successful?**

* High school diploma in administration or logistics, or vocational school diploma with at least two years of experience in accounting/administrative roles within Warehouse and/or Shipping environments.
* Knowledge of administrative and accounting procedures, preferably including customs\-related aspects, within Warehouse and Shipping operations.
* At least one year of experience in administrative and accounting activities in Warehouse and Shipping.
* User\-level knowledge of information management systems, including ERP (Oracle) and Microsoft Office.
* Written and spoken English proficiency, required to interact with colleagues and departments from different countries.
* Knowledge of Warehouse Management Systems (WMS), preferably Stock System.
Knowledge of safety, environmental, and quality regulations and procedures in Warehouse and Shipping environments.  
* 

If **YOU** are the person we are looking for, feel free to apply and let's start drafting your future career in a healthy and growing environment!  



The successful candidate will embrace Vertiv’s Core Principals \& Behaviors to help execute our Strategic Priorities.  



**OUR CORE PRINCIPALS** : Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity \& Inclusion.  



**OUR STRATEGIC PRIORITIES**

* Customer Focus
* Operational Excellence
* High\-Performance Culture
* Innovation
Financial Strength  
* 

**OUR BEHAVIORS**

* Own It
* Act With Urgency
* Foster a Customer\-First Mindset
* Think Big and Execute
* Lead by Example
* Drive Continuous Improvement
Learn and Seek Out Development  
* 

  

\#LI\-DP1  



Vertiv is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability. If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, you can request help by sending an email to help.join@vertivco.com . If you are interested in applying or learning more about this role, please visit the company’s career page located on Vertiv.com/Careers
company_addresses: 505 N Cleveland Ave, Westerville, Ohio, United States
company_description: Vertiv is a global leader in critical digital infrastructure. The world depends on data we power and cool™
